1_sequential_10.result: All 10 to Computer A (highest weight wins every time)

4_failover.result: 3 to A, 2 to B, 0 failovers needed
4_failover.note: Failover mechanism exists but not triggered (both nodes 100% healthy)

6_sustained_100.distribution: 44% A / 56% B (B faster, gets more traffic over time)

key_findings: 1. Both GPUs serve inference successfully across the LAN
2. Weighted random selection achieves near-perfect 50/50 distribution
3. Computer B (direct llama-server) is 1.8x faster than A (Hive-AI + RAG)
4. Under concurrent load, pool gives 1.7x throughput over single node
5. EMA scoring self-tunes: faster nodes naturally get more traffic
6. 100% success rate across 217 requests — zero failures
7. Network overhead is negligible (<1ms RTT on same switch)
8. Dual-format routing works: Hive-AI API + OpenAI-compatible llama-server
issues_found: 1. Deterministic routing (always highest weight) starved new nodes — fixed with weighted random
2. Pool router only tried /api/compute/inference — fixed with OpenAI-compatible fallback
3. Health check only tried /ready — fixed with multi-endpoint probing
